title: Nolana deflexa

intro: Nolana deflexa, commonly known as Chilean evening primrose or Nolana, is a flowering plant species native to the Andes Mountains in South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, pain, and skin issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antioxidant properties. Bioactive constituents of Nolana deflexa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal effects. Herbal preparations made from Nolana deflexa include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat conditions such as arthritis, gout, and eczema.
